stem: A pupil is running a web browser, a word processor and a music player at the same time on a 4 GB laptop.
model_answer: The operating system's memory manager allocates a region of RAM to each running program when it starts, keeps a record of which addresses each program owns, and stops one program from reading or writing to another's region. When RAM is fully used, the OS can swap out the least-recently-used pages to disk (virtual memory) so a new program can start; this is slower but the user only sees more memory available.
review_notes: Teacher-marked. Look for: the OS allocates RAM, isolates programs, and uses virtual memory / paging when RAM is full. 'OS shares the RAM' alone is too vague.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Describe how the operating system manages memory while several programs are running at once.

mark_points.text: allocates a portion of RAM to each running program
keeps programs isolated so they cannot read/write each other's memory
uses virtual memory / paging when RAM is full (swap to disk)
